reasoning: Each output performs only 64 multiply-adds on a tiny, repeatedly reused working set: about 512 B from `a` + 256 B from `b` + 4 B store for roughly 128 FLOPs, so arithmetic intensity is only about 0.17 FLOP/byte, far below the 62 FLOP/byte ridge point. However, this access pattern has extreme inter-thread redundancy (`a[base+j]` is identical for all 64 threads in a group, and every thread rereads the same 64-element `b` vector), so much of the traffic should be served by cache rather than DRAM; on A10G this implies low DRAM utilization with stalls dominated by waiting on load completion/scoreboarding rather than saturating bandwidth.

However, this access pattern has extreme inter-thread redundancy (`a[base+j]` is identical for all 64 threads in a group, and every thread rereads the same 64-element `b` vector), so much of the traffic should be served by cache rather than DRAM; on A10G this implies low DRAM utilization with stalls dominated by waiting on load completion/scoreboarding rather than saturating bandwidth.
